nodes.name: Webhook — Receive Onboarding
nodes.notes: URL: https://n8n.curcible.com/webhook/onboarding — paste this into N8N_WEBHOOK_URL in onboarding/index.html

nodes.name: Respond 200 Immediately
nodes.notes: Return 200 right away so the browser doesn't time out. Processing continues async.

nodes.name: n8n API — Create Credential
nodes.notes: Calls n8n's own REST API to store credentials encrypted in the vault. Requires N8N_API_KEY env var set in Railway.

nodes.name: Sheets — Mark Client Active
nodes.notes: Matches the row by Client ID and updates Status + Activated At columns

parameters.subject: You're live on Curcible.
parameters.message:
=
Curcible.

Workspace Active

You're live, {{ $('Code — Parse & Enrich Payload').first().json.firstName }}.

Your Curcible workspace is active. The following automations are now running for {{ $('Code — Parse & Enrich Payload').first().json.company }}:

{{ $('Code — Parse & Enrich Payload').first().json.products.map(p => { const names = { P07: ['MeetCore™', 'Meeting summaries + action items delivered by email'], P01: ['InboxCore™', 'Email triage, draft replies, daily digest'], P03: ['LeadCore™', 'Lead qualification + CRM push within 2 min'], P04: ['PostCore™', 'Social content published from your Airtable calendar'], P02: ['ContentCore™', 'SEO blog articles from a keyword in under 3 min'], P05: ['ReportCore™', 'Monday morning analytics digest'], P06: ['KnowCore™', 'WhatsApp AI chatbot answering customer questions'], P08: ['SchedCore™', 'Booking confirmations + reminders automated'] }; const [name, desc] = names[p] || [p, '']; return ``; }).join('') }}
Product What it does
${name}${desc}

Your {{ $('Code — Parse & Enrich Payload').first().json.tier.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + $('Code — Parse & Enrich Payload').first().json.tier.slice(1) }} plan is billed at ${{ $('Code — Parse & Enrich Payload').first().json.mrr }}/month.

If anything looks off or you need to adjust a credential, reply to this email.

Curcible · support@curcible.com

parameters.subject: =New client onboarded: {{ $('Code — Parse & Enrich Payload').first().json.company }} ({{ $('Code — Parse & Enrich Payload').first().json.tier }})

ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.N8N_API_KEY: Generate in n8n: Settings > API > Create API Key

PLACEHOLDERS_TO_REPLACE: {{CURCIBLE_SHEETS_ID}} — Google Sheets spreadsheet ID (the long string in the URL: docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/THIS_PART/edit)
{{CURCIBLE_GOOGLE_CRED_ID}} — n8n credential ID for Curcible's own Google OAuth2 (used for both Sheets + Gmail)
{{CURCIBLE_GMAIL_CRED_ID}} — n8n credential ID for Curcible's own Gmail (support@curcible.com) — can be same as CURCIBLE_GOOGLE_CRED_ID
{{CURCIBLE_OWNER_EMAIL}} — your email to receive new client notifications
{{TEMPLATE_WORKFLOW_ID_P07}} — n8n workflow ID of your P07 template (find via GET /api/v1/workflows)

GOOGLE_SHEETS_SETUP.notes: Create a new Google Sheet, rename Sheet1 to 'Clients', add these headers in row 1 exactly as written. The workflow matches on Client ID to update the row status.

ACTIVATION_FLOW_EXPLAINED: 3. Payload is parsed and a client record is appended to your Google Sheet (Clients tab)
4. Each credential the client provided is created in n8n's encrypted vault via the n8n REST API
5. For each product in the client's tier, the template workflow is fetched
6. The template is cloned: renamed, credential IDs swapped, CLIENT_ID placeholders replaced
7. The cloned workflow is saved and activated via n8n API
8. Google Sheet row is updated: Status → Active, Activated At → now
9. Client receives 'You're live' email listing their active products
IMPORTANT_NOTES: Enable the n8n REST API in: Settings > API > Enable API
The workflow handles all 8 products — only the ones in the client's tier are activated
Template workflows must be imported first (use the P0X-*.json files) and set to INACTIVE before use as templates
The loop over products (steps 5–7) uses n8n's standard item processing — each product triggers its own Get/Clone/Create/Activate chain
Credentials with multiple keys (e.g. Zoom needs both client_id and client_secret) are handled in Code — Build Credential Objects
